content: Management depends on risk and life expectancy. Very low/low risk, life expectancy >10 years: active surveillance (AS) preferred - PSA every 3-6 months, DRE annually, repeat biopsy every 1-3 years; curative treatment if reclassification. Intermediate risk: radiation therapy (EBRT or brachytherapy) + short-term ADT (4-6 months) or radical prostatectomy; some favorable intermediate may choose AS. High/very high risk: radiation + long-term ADT (18-36 months) or radical prostatectomy with pelvic lymph node dissection; consider adding abiraterone for very high risk (STAMPEDE trial). Metastatic hormone-sensitive: ADT + intensification - docetaxel (CHAARTED), abiraterone (LATITUDE), apalutamide (TITAN), or enzalutamide (ENZAMET); triplet therapy (ADT + docetaxel + darolutamide/abiraterone) for high-volume disease (ARASENS, PEACE-1). Castration-resistant: non-metastatic - apalutamide, enzalutamide, or darolutamide (add to ADT); metastatic - docetaxel, cabazitaxel, abiraterone, enzalutamide, radium-223 (bone-predominant), lutetium-177-PSMA (VISION trial), PARP inhibitors for HRR-mutated (PROfound, TRITON3).
